explanation.summary: Carbs are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in S tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in S tier with an overall score of 53 from a base nutrition score of 50. Context lifted it by 3 points.

topPros.title: elite micronutrient density
topPros.explanation: This is exactly the kind of nutrient-packed vegetable the category is supposed to reward.

topCons.title: contains anti-nutrients
topCons.explanation: Oxalates can reduce mineral usefulness in some contexts.

topCons.title: volume shrinks a lot when cooked
topCons.explanation: Can make portions feel smaller than expected.

topCons.title: not useful as a fuel food
topCons.explanation: That is fine for vegetables, but it does limit some use cases.

narrationNotes: Context improved the final tier case by 3 points.

explanation.summary: Fats are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in B tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in B tier with an overall score of 28 from a base nutrition score of 25. Context lifted it by 3 points.

topPros.title: very strong vitamin A identity
topPros.explanation: This is a major category strength and part of what makes carrots a genuinely good vegetable.

topCons.title: sweetness is a bit higher than the cleanest vegetables
topCons.explanation: Still fine, just not ultra-low-sugar by category standards.

topCons.title: micronutrient spread is narrower than elite greens
topCons.explanation: Great at some things, less absurdly dense overall than the very top vegetables.

topCons.title: protein is basically irrelevant
topCons.explanation: Not a real flaw, but still nearly absent.

narrationNotes: Context improved the final tier case by 3 points.

explanation.summary: Fats are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in D tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in D tier with an overall score of 12 from a base nutrition score of 21. Context dragged it down by 9 points.

topPros.title: adds hydrating volume
topPros.explanation: It can bulk out meals with almost no calories.

topPros.title: easy salad base
topPros.explanation: Very practical in sandwiches, burgers, and salads.

topPros.title: mild and easy to tolerate
topPros.explanation: A practical plus for people who dislike bitter greens.

topCons.title: micronutrient payoff is weak
topCons.explanation: That is a major problem in a category meant to reward nutrient density.

topCons.title: fibre payoff is weak for a vegetable
topCons.explanation: It does not offer much of the maintenance-and-repair value the category is supposed to reward.

topCons.title: mostly water with limited nutritional return
topCons.explanation: Very low payoff compared with stronger vegetables.

narrationNotes: Context weakened the final tier case by 9 points.
